executive_summary.narrative: This release surfaces a confirmed live secret, a SQL injection vulnerability, and multiple critical/high dependency CVEs across both backend and frontend — the application-security domain this pipeline rarely gets to assess given how often these scans are skipped.

top_risks.title: Live secret confirmed in commit history
top_risks.impact: GitGuardian confirmed this credential is currently valid, not just pattern-matched.
top_risks.why_it_matters: An active credential in git history is immediately exploitable by anyone with repo access.
top_risks.recommended_action: Revoke the credential immediately and purge it from git history.

top_risks.title: SQL injection in order routes
top_risks.impact: Unsanitized user input reaches a SQL query.
top_risks.why_it_matters: Direct path to data exfiltration or corruption.

priority_actions.title: Revoke and rotate the exposed credential
priority_actions.rationale: Highest-impact, lowest-effort fix — a single rotation closes the most urgent exposure.

priority_actions.title: Upgrade Flask past both known CVEs
priority_actions.rationale: Two separate CVEs share the same pinned Flask version — one upgrade resolves both.
priority_actions.expected_risk_reduction: Removes both flagged Flask CVEs in one change.

release_readiness.rationale: A confirmed live secret plus an unmitigated SQL injection are each independently sufficient to block this release.
